fields.name: To Kill a Mockingbird
fields.description: Harper Lee's Pulitzer Prize-winning masterpiece about racial injustice and moral growth in the American South
George Orwell's chilling dystopian novel about a totalitarian society and the dangers of absolute power

fields.name: Pride and Prejudice
fields.description: Jane Austen's beloved romantic novel following Elizabeth Bennet and the proud Mr. Darcy

fields.name: The Great Gatsby
fields.description: F. Scott Fitzgerald's portrait of the Jazz Age and the elusive American Dream

fields.name: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
fields.description: J.K. Rowling's magical tale of a young wizard discovering his destiny at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry

fields.name: The Catcher in the Rye
fields.description: J.D. Salinger's iconic coming-of-age novel narrated by the rebellious Holden Caulfield

fields.name: The Alchemist
fields.description: Paulo Coelho's philosophical novel about a shepherd boy's journey to find his personal legend

fields.name: Of Mice and Men
fields.description: John Steinbeck's poignant novella about the friendship of two displaced ranch workers during the Great Depression

fields.name: Beloved
fields.description: Toni Morrison's Pulitzer Prize-winning novel about the haunting legacy of slavery in post-Civil War America

fields.name: The Color Purple
fields.description: Alice Walker's epistolary novel about the struggles and triumphs of African American women in the rural South

fields.name: One Hundred Years of Solitude
fields.description: Gabriel García Márquez's landmark magical realist novel tracing seven generations of the Buendía family

fields.name: The Road
fields.description: Cormac McCarthy's harrowing post-apocalyptic novel about a father and son's struggle to survive

fields.name: Atomic Habits
fields.description: James Clear's practical guide to building good habits and breaking bad ones through small, incremental changes

fields.name: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People
fields.description: Stephen R. Covey's enduring guide to personal and professional effectiveness through principle-centered living

fields.name: Thinking, Fast and Slow
fields.description: Daniel Kahneman's exploration of the two systems that drive the way we think and make decisions

fields.name: Outliers
fields.description: Malcolm Gladwell's fascinating look at what makes high-achievers successful, from the Beatles to Bill Gates

fields.name: The Body Keeps the Score
fields.description: Bessel van der Kolk's groundbreaking work on how trauma reshapes body and brain, and paths to recovery

fields.name: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
fields.description: Yuval Noah Harari's sweeping narrative of humanity's past and its implications for our future

fields.name: Quiet: The Power of Introverts
fields.description: Susan Cain's revolutionary examination of introversion and how introverts can harness their strengths

fields.name: Blink
fields.description: Malcolm Gladwell's exploration of rapid cognition and the power of thinking without thinking

fields.name: Gone Girl
fields.description: Gillian Flynn's twisty psychological thriller about a marriage gone terrifyingly wrong

fields.name: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
fields.description: Stieg Larsson's international bestseller about a journalist and hacker investigating a decades-old disappearance

fields.name: Big Little Lies
fields.description: Liane Moriarty's darkly comic novel about three women whose lives intertwine around a murder investigation

fields.name: In the Woods
fields.description: Tana French's haunting debut novel about a detective investigating a murder near the site of a childhood trauma

fields.name: The Da Vinci Code
fields.description: Dan Brown's global phenomenon following Harvard symbologist Robert Langdon through a deadly conspiracy

fields.name: And Then There Were None
fields.description: Agatha Christie's world-famous mystery in which ten strangers are lured to an island and begin to die one by one

fields.name: The Girl on the Train
fields.description: Paula Hawkins' gripping psychological thriller about an obsessed commuter entangled in a missing person case

fields.name: Sharp Objects
fields.description: Gillian Flynn's debut novel about a troubled journalist returning to her hometown to cover a series of murders

fields.name: Dune
fields.description: Frank Herbert's epic science fiction novel set on the desert planet Arrakis, the universe's only source of spice

fields.name: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
fields.description: Douglas Adams' hilarious comedic sci-fi adventure that begins with the destruction of Earth

fields.name: Ender's Game
fields.description: Orson Scott Card's classic military science fiction about a child prodigy trained to fight an alien invasion

fields.name: A Game of Thrones
fields.description: George R.R. Martin's sweeping fantasy epic of power, betrayal, and survival in the Seven Kingdoms

fields.name: The Hunger Games
fields.description: Suzanne Collins' dystopian tale of Katniss Everdeen forced to compete in a televised fight to the death

fields.name: Brave New World
fields.description: Aldous Huxley's prophetic dystopian novel imagining a future where pleasure and conformity control society

fields.name: Fahrenheit 451
fields.description: Ray Bradbury's classic about a future society where books are banned and firemen burn them

fields.name: Neuromancer
fields.description: William Gibson's groundbreaking cyberpunk novel that defined the genre and coined the term 'cyberspace'

fields.name: The Left Hand of Darkness
fields.description: Ursula K. Le Guin's Hugo and Nebula Award-winning novel exploring gender and society on an alien world

fields.name: Foundation
fields.description: Isaac Asimov's visionary series opener about a mathematician who creates a plan to preserve civilization

fields.name: Educated
fields.description: Tara Westover's memoir of growing up in a survivalist family in rural Idaho and her journey to Cambridge

fields.name: Becoming
fields.description: Michelle Obama's intimate and inspiring memoir about her remarkable journey from the South Side of Chicago to the White House

fields.name: The Glass Castle
fields.description: Jeannette Walls' remarkable memoir of growing up with eccentric, nomadic parents and her path to success

fields.name: I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings
fields.description: Maya Angelou's autobiographical account of her childhood and the formative experiences that shaped her identity

fields.name: Born a Crime
fields.description: Trevor Noah's funny and affecting memoir about growing up in apartheid South Africa as a mixed-race child

fields.name: When Breath Becomes Air
fields.description: Paul Kalanithi's moving memoir about facing a terminal diagnosis and searching for the meaning of a life well-lived

fields.name: Wild
fields.description: Cheryl Strayed's memoir of hiking the Pacific Crest Trail alone after the collapse of her life

fields.name: The Diary of a Young Girl
fields.description: Anne Frank's world-famous diary documenting her life in hiding during the Nazi occupation of the Netherlands

fields.name: Alexander Hamilton
fields.description: Ron Chernow's definitive biography of the Founding Father whose turbulent life inspired the hit Broadway musical

fields.name: Guns, Germs, and Steel
fields.description: Jared Diamond's Pulitzer Prize-winning analysis of why Western civilizations came to dominate the world

fields.name: The Devil in the White City
fields.description: Erik Larson's gripping true-crime account of the 1893 World's Fair and a serial killer operating in its shadow

fields.name: Team of Rivals
fields.description: Doris Kearns Goodwin's acclaimed biography of Abraham Lincoln and the political genius that united his cabinet
